author: @vgman94

text: This is probably the funniest Sonic game. I love Sonic's entrance and his reaction to just about everything. The dialog between him and Caliburn is priceless. I think the Japanese writers totally nailed it with this game and Unleashed. It's the perfect balance of silly and serious. Shame they got replaced by the MadWorld writers... It seems like we were /this close/ to finally getting some proper cartoon humor in a Sonic game (with emphasis on proper).
author: @BaconStein

text: @vgman94 I wouldn't say the last... I'm still confident that whatever Dimps has to contribute to the series could be good. I find Sonic 4 and Colors (DS version) thoroughly enjoyable for the most part. Last great 3D game, maybe. I definitely miss Sonic's old personality and the imagination that went into these games. Unleashed and Black Knight especially have a very strong presentation, writing, and sense of atmosphere. Things just haven't been the same since Colors.

text: @SuperSketch008 I did too. Moreso with it's sibling franchise, Crash Bandicoot, but I grew up with both and Sonic. I call them "the Big 3" for their similarities. The Future series is very similar, but more story-heavy and has a few innovations, particularly noticeable in Tools of Destruction and A Crack In Time. But it's still mainly the same stuff, which makes me baffled when some people complain about it being too different. Well, I don't like 06' much, but I wouldn't call it complete crap.
author: @vgman94

text: @SuperSketch008 You've heard of them? Wow. Cool. Not many people know about those games. I suppose. Unnecessary to an extent, but it wrapped up Shadow's saga nicely, at least. You do have a point with some of it's darker themes which aren't even delved into as much in LOS and Ratchet & Clank. I think it was only Sonic's romance that felt like some fanfic. Black Knight felt like a half-finished, functional, but underdeveloped gimmick. They just needed more time with it, I believe.
author: @vgman94

text: @PonineTeazerBlaze No. Sonic 06' had them together as well, but not together as in interacting. I never saw them talk t each other, even one line.
author: @vgman94

text: @SuperSketch008 I disagree. Shadow's game was darker because it's, well, Shadow's game. SA2 was great and had a great plot. It and Black Knight have my favorite tone of plot, but I think there can be exceptions. Secret Rings wasn't too serious, and 06' was alright except for the romance crap. Anthropomorphic or not, that doesn't draw a very important line on how serious it can be. Some stuff like The Legend of Spyro trilogy and Ratchet & Clank Future trilogy make it work perfectly fine.
author: @vgman94

text: @SuperSketch008 Improperly? How? The gun and sword ideas were unfinished, but not wrongly executed. I do agree there. If Generations keeps reusing the same old crap over and over (it looks like Sonic 4 and Unleashed day combined...) and SEGA keeps this direction, we'll know Sonic has died. Hell, as I said in my first comment, I think Sonic hasn't be truly alive since this game. Whatever your opinion is on the gimmicks, at least they TRIED something new. Well, SEGA did need some inspiration.
author: @vgman94

text: @SuperSketch008 No. Sonic innovated and added new ideas each game, had good attempts at a story and in this and Unleashed in particular, had a lot of side content. Now what we have is running with a few obstacles for 7 hours and little story with gimmicky side content (and not in a good way. It;s overused, "classic" side missions). It's like all people want is low-quality, rehashed games instead of something new to see. Sonic was far more than a simple kid platformer, once.
